known_issues: Game won't load initially and won't reload when refresh is attempted

existing_features.title: Implement a 3D first-person shooter game environment using React Three Fiber with controllable player movement (WASD keys for forward/backward/strafe, mouse for looking around)
Add shooting mechanics with visual feedback including muzzle flash, projectile trajectories, and hit detection against targets
Create a 3D game arena with obstacles, cover positions, and environmental geometry for tactical gameplay
Implement enemy targets or NPCs that can be destroyed when shot, with visual feedback for hits and destruction
Add game state management tracking player health, ammunition count, score, and enemy defeats
Display HUD elements showing crosshair, health bar, ammunition counter, and current score
Apply a timeless, ethereal visual aesthetic with muted colors, atmospheric lighting, and a sense of suspended time to create a distinctive art style
Add console logging to the Game component to verify component mounting and initialization sequence, including Canvas setup, Physics provider initialization, and player spawn confirmation
Add error boundaries around the Canvas and Game components to catch and display any rendering errors that might cause a blank screen
Add a loading state indicator that displays while the 3D scene and assets are initializing to prevent confusion about blank screens during load time
Verify that the Internet Identity authentication flow does not block the game from rendering, and ensure the game displays regardless of authentication state
Investigate and fix the issue preventing the game from loading on initial page load, ensuring the 3D canvas and game components render successfully
Fix the page reload functionality so that refreshing the browser successfully reloads the game instead of failing or getting stuck
Add comprehensive error logging and debugging information to identify what prevents loading and reloading, including React lifecycle events, Three.js initialization, and Physics provider setup
Verify that the React Three Fiber Canvas, Physics provider, and all 3D components initialize correctly without race conditions or dependency issues that could cause loading failures

Add detailed error tracking to capture and log the specific load error occurring on deployment, including stack traces, component mount failures, and any React Three Fiber or Physics initialization errors
Verify that all required dependencies for React Three Fiber Canvas and Physics provider are properly loaded before attempting to render the 3D scene
Add fallback rendering logic that displays a meaningful error message to the user if the 3D canvas fails to initialize, rather than showing a blank screen
Implement retry logic that automatically attempts to reinitialize the game if the initial load fails, with a maximum of 3 retry attempts before showing a permanent error state
Ensure the ErrorBoundary component properly catches and displays errors from the Canvas, Physics provider, and all child 3D components with detailed error information
